title: TRIMET TICKETS - 211info
text: TRANSITION PROJECTS TRIMET TICKETS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Bus or MAX tickets are available for people starting a new, permanent, full-time job or for medical appointments. Tickets are only provided for the first week of the new job. 10-ticket maximum every 90 days. No long-distance transportation assistance offered. 650 NW Irving Street Portland, OR 97209 Eligibility: Individuals experiencing homelessness and other very low- to no-income adults. Only available for people starting a new, permanent job or for medical appointments. Hours: Resource Center (in person or phone): Monday-Friday 8:30am-7pm, Saturday/Sunday/all holidays 8:30am-3pm; service intakes end 15 minutes before close (503) 280-4700 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 280-4700 INTAKE PROCEDURE: Walk in to Resource Center, call, or visit website for more information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: Bring phone number to verify employment or medical appointment LANGUAGES: English, Spanish AREA SERVED: Multnomah County TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Two blocks northwest of Union Station. On theTriMet MAX Yellow and Green lines. Services: Transportation Passes Other Services Offered At This Location: CONSUMER REGULATION - IDENTIFICATION ASSISTANCE GENERAL MEDICAL CARE - MEDICAL CLINIC HOUSING/SHELTER - ARGYLE GARDENS HOUSING/SHELTER - BARBARA MAHER APARTMENTS HOUSING/SHELTER - CLARK CENTER HOUSING/SHELTER - DOREEN'S PLACE HOUSING/SHELTER - JEAN'S PLACE HOUSING/SHELTER - RESOURCE CENTER If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
